title: thelypteris palustris subsp. pubescens
intro: Thelypteris palustris subsp. pubescens, also known as the southern lady fern, is a subspecies of fern found in the southeastern United States. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thelypteris palustris subsp. pubescens include potential relief from arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and to lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the southern lady fern can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and to promote a healthy digestive system.
